text.title: Behavior Parity Verification Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes seen when solo developers run shadow parity tests, especially with AI-written normalizers. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: PII (emails, names, tokens) lands in the diff store unredacted.
failure-mode.detector: Sample 100 rows from parity_diffs; grep for "@", common name patterns, or token prefixes ("sk_", "tok_"). If any match without hashing, contract violated.
failure-mode.repair: Add a redaction pass in the diff sampler BEFORE persistence. Wipe the existing store. Document the redaction list in the parity report.

failure-mode.description: Timestamp false-positives dominate diff rate. The normalizer leaves request-time or server-rendered times in observable fields.
failure-mode.detector: diff_rate > 5% AND top cluster shows only timestamp-shaped values diverging
failure-mode.repair: Move the timestamp fields out of observables OR canonicalize them (round to second, replace with placeholder). Re-run from current ramp stage with the fix.

failure-mode.description: Ramp jumped from 1% to 100% without intermediate stages because "diff rate was zero."
failure-mode.detector: parity-report stage sequence does not include 10% AND 50% records OR sample count at 1% < 1000
failure-mode.repair: Re-do staged ramp. Zero diffs at 1% with 100 requests is uninformative — many real bugs only fire on input shapes that occur once per 10k requests.

failure-mode.description: Async side-effects (queued jobs, emitted events) are not on the observable list, so the new path silently fires duplicate or missing emails / webhooks.
failure-mode.detector: Service emits events to a queue OR DB triggers, AND observable list contains only synchronous response fields
failure-mode.repair: Add event/job observables: compare queue-message bodies between legacy and new with the same normalization rules. Use a no-op consumer in shadow to capture without acting.

failure-mode.description: Normalizer is permissive enough to mask real diffs (e.g., tolerance set to "any int within 100" of legacy total).
failure-mode.detector: tolerance > 0.1% of value, OR normalizer rewrites entire field to a constant
failure-mode.repair: Tighten tolerance to the business-meaningful threshold (cents for money, milliseconds for SLAs). If tolerance must be wide, document why and treat the field as untested.

failure-mode.description: Cluster analysis is skipped — diff samples are stored but never grouped, so the team ramps past stage 2 with hundreds of unread diff rows.
failure-mode.detector: parity_diffs count at stage_end > 10 AND no cluster table in parity-report
failure-mode.repair: Run `scripts/parity-summarize.py`. For each cluster: produce root cause OR write an accepted-divergence justification. No silent acceptance.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: AI agents writing the normalizer must not invent canonicalization rules that change semantic meaning ("strip negative signs to match", "replace nulls with zero"). Such rules MUST be flagged and human-reviewed before deployment; they are silent semantic rewrites, not normalization.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Sign-off on the final 100% parity report is the human gate. The developer (or a peer reviewer) confirms: zero unresolved clusters, all accepted divergences have written justification, error rate at parity with legacy. Only then is the legacy code path scheduled for removal.
